pattern: The shortfall between the current sum and the goal is closed greedily by elements each carrying the maximum allowed magnitude, so the answer is a ceiling division.
hints: Only the gap between the present sum and the goal matters, not the individual elements already present.
Each added element can contribute at most 'limit' in magnitude toward closing that gap.
Compute the absolute difference between goal and the current sum, then divide it by limit rounding up to get the minimum count.

edge_inputs.description: needs two negative additions to reach a negative goal
single large element closes the gap to zero
already at goal needs no additions
gap exactly equals limit needs exactly one element
gap one over limit needs two elements
positive sum overshoots a smaller goal, fill with negatives
very large gap with limit one

stress.description: random array, random limit and goal across the full ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    limit = rng.randrange(1, 1000001)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-limit, limit + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    goal = rng.randrange(-1000000000, 1000000001)
    return [nums, limit, goal]
